text.title: SOC2 Evidence Generator CLI — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every `faion soc2 evidence` invocation must enforce. These are HARD rules — developer or CI configuration cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every merged PR touching files declared in-scope by the control map MUST emit a stub; the merge action fails closed if stub generation errors.
rule.rationale: Missing evidence on a merged change is the single most common audit finding; failing-closed prevents the gap from forming.

rule.statement: The stub MUST contain: control_ids[], actor (verified Git identity), merge_commit_sha, diff_hash (SHA-256 of the unified diff), reviewer_approvals[], ci_run_ids[], merged_at (UTC ISO 8601), and a cryptographic signature over the canonical JSON.
rule.rationale: Auditors challenge any field they cannot independently verify; the seven fields cover the standard chain-of-custody questions.

rule.statement: Control labels MUST come from the closed vocabulary defined in the upstream `soc2-control-to-repo-artifact-map`; unknown labels reject the stub and surface the diff for relabeling.
rule.rationale: Free-form labels produce orphan stubs that the binder cannot aggregate; closed vocab forces the conversation before merge.

rule.statement: Stubs MUST be written to an append-only store with immutability guarantees (S3 Object Lock, immutable bucket, git ref under signed-tag protection); deletion of any past stub fires a P0 alert.

rationale.source: SOC 2 CC7.2 detection of unauthorized changes

rule.rationale: Evidence whose history can be rewritten silently is functionally absent; immutability is the only credible defense.

rule.statement: The CLI MUST output a stable, sortable evidence_id (e.g., `evid_{merged_at}_{short_sha}`) and MUST be idempotent: re-running on the same merge_commit_sha returns the original evidence_id without writing a duplicate.
rule.rationale: Webhook retries and re-runs are a fact of life; non-idempotent emit produces duplicate evidence that confuses the auditor and inflates storage cost.

example.title: Anchored application
example.note: Closed-vocab labels, signed, idempotent id, append-only target. The auditor runs one query and gets the change story in seconds.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: PR #482 merged. See Slack #compliance for context.
example.note: No control mapping, no signed integrity, no reviewer record. The auditor cannot verify any claim; the finding writes itself.
